title: NPC Motivations
description: The driving want, fear, or obligation behind an NPC's behavior — usable as a one-line character engine for any genre.

entries.result: Wants to prove something to a person who no longer cares.
Is trying to earn enough to leave this place permanently.
Is protecting someone who doesn't know they need protecting.
Needs to repay a debt before it becomes dangerous.
Is looking for information about a family member who disappeared.
Has a secret that, if discovered, would destroy their reputation.
Is trying to undo a mistake they made years ago.
Wants recognition they've earned but never received.
Is being blackmailed and is running out of ways to comply.
Has recently lost everything and is rebuilding from the lowest point.
Is loyal to a person or institution they privately know to be corrupt.
Wants revenge for something that happened a long time ago and isn't sure they still mean it.
Is afraid of dying obscure and wants to matter before they go.
Owes a favor that will eventually be collected in a way they can't predict.
Has a dream they've given up on and meets every reminder of it with defensiveness.
Is trying to find the person who wronged them before their health fails.
Is acting against their own interests to protect someone they love.
Wants to be left alone and is failing at achieving this.
Is maintaining a persona they're no longer sure they believe in.
Has been given a task they didn't choose and can't refuse.
Wants to see one specific thing happen before they die, regardless of the cost.
Is searching for proof that something they witnessed was real.
Is quietly transferring power or resources to someone else and hoping no one notices.
Needs to discredit someone before that person can discredit them.
Has dedicated their life to a cause that has already won — and hasn't found a new one.
Is trying to broker peace between two parties who don't know they need it.
Believes something that no one else believes and keeps finding evidence that seems to confirm it.
Is waiting for permission to do something they've already decided to do.
Wants someone to take their place in an obligation they didn't choose.
Is completing what someone else started, out of loyalty rather than belief.
Has information that would help many people but would harm the one they love most.
Is trying to disappear from a situation they created.
Needs something the party has and is trying to avoid asking for it.
Is building something that won't be understood or appreciated for decades.
Has outlived everyone they cared about and is now unsure what they're doing it for.
Wants to confess something but hasn't decided who to confess to.
Is trying to acquire enough influence to protect someone specific from someone powerful.
Has a plan they're too proud to abandon even though it's failing.
Is trying to document something before it disappears.
Needs to be somewhere specific by a specific time and is running out of time.
Is serving someone who doesn't deserve their loyalty and is beginning to know it.
Wants to be forgiven but refuses to ask for forgiveness.
Is keeping a promise that was made under circumstances that no longer apply.
Has been given a second chance and is terrified of wasting it.
Is trying to locate something lost before someone else finds it first.
Wants to finish something they started long ago before their ability to do so fades.
Is holding a position of responsibility they no longer want but cannot hand off.
Has a grievance against an institution and is working through legitimate channels, slowly, with great patience.
Is accumulating favors as currency for a future need they haven't named yet.
Wants to know what happened and will not stop asking until they find out.
Is maintaining a lie that has grown too large to safely abandon.
Has inherited a responsibility they didn't understand when they accepted it.
Wants to be the person their mentor believed they were.
Is trying to make something right without admitting they made it wrong.
Has dedicated themselves to a person rather than a cause and goes where that person goes.
Is afraid of what they might become if they stop working.
Needs to deliver a message they've been carrying for years.
Is trying to determine if they can trust someone before they have to act on that trust.
Has a standing offer they can call on once and is waiting for the right moment.
Is building a body of work that will prove a theory that has already been dismissed.
Wants to die with a specific thing resolved.
Is trying to determine who among their allies is betraying them.
Has refused a calling for years and is slowly losing the argument with themselves.
Is moving something or someone to safety under conditions they can't fully control.
Wants to be believed about something no one will believe.
Is acquiring something specific piece by piece, over years, toward an eventual whole.
Is loyal to a dead person's wishes and uncertain whether those wishes still apply.
Has a talent they've hidden because the last time they used it, something went wrong.
Wants to give a specific person the chance they didn't get.
Is trying to find out who is responsible for something without tipping off the responsible party.
Has promised to return somewhere and hasn't yet found the courage.
Is trying to keep two parts of their life from touching.
Wants to witness something before it ends — a place, a person, an era.
Is preparing for a crisis only they can see coming.
Has turned their grief into a project and is living inside the project.
Is working to restore something that was taken from a community, not from themselves.
Has a plan that requires one specific thing the party might provide.
Wants to stop doing what they do but hasn't been given permission by anyone who matters to them.
Is paying off a debt that is not their own and is not being repaid by the person who owes it.
Is trying to stay out of something that they were once central to.
Has a loyalty to a place rather than a person, and will not leave it.
Wants to find out if the story they've been telling about their own life is true.
Is searching for a replacement for a thing or person that cannot be replaced.
Is trying to protect their reputation in the only place that reputation still matters.
Has been told to wait and is waiting, though the person who told them may never come back.
Is trying to outrun consequences that are moving faster than they are.
Has accepted that they will not survive what they're doing and is doing it anyway.
Wants recognition from a specific person and no recognition from anyone else matters.
Is doing a job that requires them to act against their own values, slowly and incrementally.
Is trying to understand why something happened and cannot move forward until they do.
Has an obligation to a community that requires them to be present even when they want to leave.
Is gathering the pieces of something that was deliberately scattered.
Wants to make something that will outlast them.
Is trying to prevent an event that they may have set in motion themselves.
Has been wrongly credited with something and is waiting to be found out.
Is trying to get close to someone they need to confront.
Has one last thing to do before they allow themselves to rest.
Is watching for the right moment to act on a decision already made.
Wants to know what happened to someone they loved who simply vanished.
Is trying to be someone different and is not sure yet whether they're succeeding.
Has made a promise that puts them in conflict with every other commitment in their life.
Wants to find proof that their sacrifice mattered.
Is carrying someone else's unfinished business and has been for too long.
Needs to stop someone without becoming the thing they're trying to stop.
Is working backward from a future they're afraid of, trying to prevent it.
Has given up one thing for every other thing they've ever wanted, and keeps losing the count.
Is trying to reach someone who has closed themselves off completely.
Wants to hold on long enough for something to change.
Is attempting to undo an alliance they made at a lower point in their life.
Has an opportunity they're not sure they deserve and is waiting to see if they take it.
Is filling a role left empty by someone's death and living inside the comparison.
Wants a particular truth spoken out loud by the person who knows it.
Is managing a declining situation as gracefully as possible for as long as possible.
Has made peace with their fate but not with the people who caused it.
Is looking for someone who can do what they cannot and teach it to them before it's too late.
Wants to be certain before they act and the time for certainty is running out.
Is trying to restore a relationship that they damaged and that the other person has not forgiven.
Has an old wound that still controls more decisions than they'll admit.
Is trying to achieve through legitimate means what others in their position take by force.
Wants to leave something for the people who come after, even if they never know about it.
Has been waiting to be asked for something only they can provide.
Is trying to find the right moment to withdraw from a conflict they've been central to.
Has one person's wellbeing as their entire organizing principle.
Is looking for something to believe in after losing the thing they used to believe in.
Wants to be in control of something, anything, after years of having no control at all.
Is trying to hold a community together through a crisis it doesn't know is happening.
Has accepted something they once fought against and is still adjusting to having done so.
Is trying to complete something before someone else completes it first and takes the credit.
Wants to protect something that cannot protect itself.
Is trying to honor a tradition that may no longer serve the people it was meant to serve.
Has an unusual competence and a very specific application for it that they're still arranging.
Is preparing for an ending they think is inevitable, making it as clean as possible.
Wants to be remembered accurately, not heroically.
Is trying to acquire independence from a person or institution they've depended on too long.
Has said yes to too many people and is running out of ways to honor all of them.
Is protecting an idea rather than a person — and it makes them harder to reason with.
Wants to stop pretending that things are fine before they have to.
Is making decisions for the long term in a situation everyone else is managing for the short term.
Has one name they still want to clear.
Is working to give someone else a better outcome than they had.
Wants proof that their version of events is the true one.
Is holding a community secret that has become a weight they no longer want.
Is trying to remain relevant in a situation where they're being moved aside.
Has identified a problem no one else is taking seriously and is trying to be taken seriously.
Wants to settle somewhere and stay, after a life of never staying.
Is rebuilding an identity after the one they had was stripped away.
Needs to reach a specific destination before a specific event, and the margin is shrinking.
Is trying to maintain a neutral position in a conflict that keeps demanding they choose.
Has a plan that depends on no one knowing it exists.
Wants to tell someone the truth but can't find the right words or the right moment.
Is looking for something they lost so long ago they've half-convinced themselves it didn't exist.
Has a code they live by, and the situation keeps creating exceptions.
Is trying to earn back something that cannot be earned back.
Has a long-running disagreement with the way things are done and is finally in a position to change it.
Is trying to learn whether someone they've been loyal to deserves that loyalty.
Wants to prevent the next generation from making the same mistakes as their own.
Is protecting the last remnant of something that used to be larger.
Has been asked to bear witness to something and is taking that obligation seriously.
Wants one honest conversation with a specific person, and has been unable to have it.
Is maintaining a position that benefits others at ongoing personal cost.
Has put their hope in a specific outcome and is arranging everything around it.
Is trying to contain a situation that keeps growing.
Wants to leave a place they've been trapped in by circumstance rather than force.
Is trying to find out the truth about a specific historical event that shaped their life.
Has chosen the harder path because the easier one cost someone else too much.
Is trying to hold two incompatible loyalties at the same time.
Wants to get through this without losing more than they've already lost.
Is moving toward something uncertain because staying in place is worse.
Has accepted a cost and is paying it, one day at a time, without complaint.
Wants to finish this conversation, this chapter, this obligation — and breathe.
Is trying to live up to a standard that was set by someone else and has since become their own.
Has spent so long managing a crisis that they're not sure what they'd do in its absence.
Is buying time for someone else to act.
Wants to understand something they've witnessed, even if understanding it changes nothing.
Is trying to keep a promise they made to themselves, which is the hardest kind to keep.
Has taken on someone else's burden because there was no one else to carry it.
Is trying to find out if they're capable of doing what they're considering.
Wants recognition not from the world but from one specific person who knew them before.
Is working to ensure that a specific wrong is remembered rather than forgotten.
Has made peace with a loss that everyone else still thinks they should be fighting.
Is trying to stabilize something fragile long enough for others to take over.
Wants to matter to someone before they stop being able to.
Is running from the version of themselves they're afraid they'll become if they stop running.
Has identified the one thing that would make everything else worth it.
Is trying to build something that will not need them once it is built.
Wants to know if the choice they made was right, and knows they may never find out.
Is living in the gap between who they were and who they're trying to be.
Has taken responsibility for something they could have avoided and is not looking for credit.
Is trying to find the right way to say goodbye to something they've held for too long.
Wants to stop carrying a story alone.
Is doing the right thing, slowly, badly, and without certainty, because the alternative is worse.
Has decided to trust one more person and is deciding whether the party qualifies.
Is trying to close a chapter of their life that keeps reopening.
Wants to see a specific person happy before everything else falls apart.
Is holding something together through will alone and isn't sure how much will they have left.
Has a question they can only answer by acting, so they're about to act.
Is trying to create the conditions for something good to happen after they're no longer around.
Wants to do this one thing well, even if everything else has gone badly.
Is moving toward the end of something with as much dignity as they can manage.
Has already decided. They're just waiting for the moment to arrive.
